Skip to contents
curecfr 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

Priors for the mixture-cure CFR model

curecfr_priors.Rd

Priors are placed on the interpretable onset-to-death delay mean and standard deviation (in days) and on the CFR, so they do not depend on the chosen delay family. Defaults are the BDBV/Ebolavirus values used by the Julia reference model: an onset-to-death delay centred on the Isiro 2012 line-list reanalysis (mean ~= 12.75 d, sd ~= 7 d) and an EVD/BDBV Beta(6.6, 13.4) CFR prior (mean ~= 0.33). The delay mean and sd carry half-normal priors (truncated at zero).

Usage

curecfr_priors(
  delay_mean_mean = 12.75,
  delay_mean_sd = 3,
  delay_sd_mean = 7,
  delay_sd_sd = 2,
  cfr_a = 6.6,
  cfr_b = 13.4
)

Arguments

delay_mean_mean, delay_mean_sd

Half-normal prior on the delay mean (days).

delay_sd_mean, delay_sd_sd

Half-normal prior on the delay sd (days).

cfr_a, cfr_b

Beta prior on the case fatality ratio.

Value

A named list of prior hyperparameters for fit_cfr().

Examples

curecfr_priors(delay_mean_mean = 10)
#> $delay_mean_mean
#> [1] 10
#> 
#> $delay_mean_sd
#> [1] 3
#> 
#> $delay_sd_mean
#> [1] 7
#> 
#> $delay_sd_sd
#> [1] 2
#> 
#> $cfr_a
#> [1] 6.6
#> 
#> $cfr_b
#> [1] 13.4
#>
